prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye landlubber, listen close and I’ll tell ye why the sky be wearin’ its fine coat o’ blue. Ye see, the sun be sendin’ its beams o’ light down upon us, and that light be a mix o’ all the colors o’ the rainbow, a treasure chest o’ hues if ye will. Now, when that light hits the sky, it don’t just pass through willy-nilly—oh no. The wee particles o’ air and the like, they be scatterin’ that light every which way. But here’s the trick: the blue light, bein’ a scrappy little bugger, scatters more than the rest. It’s like it’s got a mind o’ its own and can’t sit still. So, when ye look up, ye see all that scattered blue, and that’s why the sky looks like a piece o’ the sea on a fair day. Now, if ye don’t mind, this ol’ pirate’s got a ship to plunder. Arrr!
